codeReview.summary: The feature adds focused integration proofs for the three promised m3 contracts and the current HEAD state still supports them. The reviewed tests exercise sensitive-contained enum save/get/list/save_many roundtrip with encrypted-at-rest checks, prove enum-bearing sensitive rows still roundtrip exactly while nested override rows coexist, and verify mixed save_many paths preserve auto-ensure plus wrong-context failure semantics for nested leaves.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill still leaves Windows startup handling easy to misapply. This reviewed worker attempted to execute .factory/init.sh directly via PowerShell before falling back, even though the mission/global guidance says .sh files on Windows must be treated as text only.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback for worker 318fe76a-299a-4835-88bb-17c8b5544c82 records a direct .factory/init.sh execution attempt before fallback; .factory/skills/rust-library-worker/SKILL.md step 2 mentions not executing .sh directly but does not explicitly restate the expected read-and-translate startup flow.

summary: Pass. The reviewed feature is valid: the added integration regressions directly cover VAL-ER-002, VAL-CROSS-003, and VAL-CROSS-005, and no blocking gaps were found in the claimed sensitive-contained enum or mixed-batch proofs.
